beats.narration: Every unexplained density change on a radiograph starts as an indication. Indication, discontinuity, defect — three words, three very different claims.

payload.title: THREE WORDS
payload.sub: Use them loosely and your report falls apart.

beats.narration: Interpretation sorts every indication. Crimp marks live on the film: false. Spatter you verify on the part: nonrelevant. The slag line? Relevant — a discontinuity.

payload.heading: One film, three different calls
payload.note: ONLY RELEVANT MOVES ON TO EVALUATION

beats.narration: A discontinuity is a fact, not a verdict. Evaluation compares it against acceptance criteria. Pass? It ships. Fail, and only then, it's a defect.
Criteria control four things — type, size, location, concentration. The same slag line can pass one code and fail the next.
So keep the ladder straight: indication — something to explain. Discontinuity — a fact. Defect — a verdict only the criteria can issue.

payload.sub: Something to explain — a fact — a verdict from the criteria.
